yesterdayAnalysis: Completed the Monday brick workout as planned: 37.1mi bike on ROUVY (TSS 71) at a conservative HR of 121 bpm, followed by two swim sessions totaling 2.0mi (TSS 28). The bike effort was appropriately controlled — well below threshold — perfect for week 8 peak volume. Combined TSS of 99 for the day represents the largest single training day of the build so far.
todayRecommendation: Execute the planned swim + Run Club session. Keep the run conversational and controlled — this is a recovery/social run between yesterday's big brick and tomorrow's Wednesday brick. Focus on movement quality and staying loose for Thursday's 9-10 mile long run.
weekProgress: Week 8 of 10: Currently at 99 TSS of 500 target (20%) with 5 days remaining including today. This is perfectly on-track given the heavily back-loaded schedule — the Saturday long ride and Wednesday brick will account for the majority of weekly volume. Monday's brick workout was completed successfully, establishing a strong foundation for the peak week.
keyInsight: Sleep quality is inconsistent (64/100 avg, 3 nights below 60) during peak week — prioritize 8+ hours and sleep hygiene to support the high training load ahead, especially before Thursday's first long run in weeks.
positives: Yesterday's 99 TSS represents the biggest single training day of the entire build — huge milestone
Bike pacing discipline excellent: HR 121 on 37mi ride shows proper Zone 2 execution aligned with Oceanside 70.3 race strategy
Seven brick workouts completed in the build demonstrates strong discipline on race-specific training
